taziri_nkhaleb.home: The road itself—feels wrong when stationary more than two days
taziri_nkhaleb.faction: Neutral merchant (minor Elunad association via late husband)
taziri_nkhaleb.appearance: Imazuran woman, late 40s, olive skin weathered by road dust, sharp brown eyes that count everything, ochre and grey traveling robes, silver measuring-chain belt, ink-stained hands
taziri_nkhaleb.and_yet: Keeps Isarna shrine in lead wagon, burns expensive incense at every crossroads
taziri_nkhaleb.wants: Profitable predictable routes, respect from both cultures, vengeance on lizard-men
taziri_nkhaleb.does_not_want: Questions about husband's death, choosing factional sides, lizard-men mentioned as allies
taziri_nkhaleb.oddly_also: Collects obscure road songs in journal, refuses water drawn south of the road
taziri_nkhaleb.thinks_with: Coin, probabilities, road-wisdom from Elunad husband
taziri_nkhaleb.family: Late husband Irven (Elunad, killed by lizard-men 8yr ago), son Khalid (24, leads return caravans), daughter Sarit (22, manages Orziben warehouses), son Davin (19, Ilmenor scribe apprentice), brother Azghar (teamster)
taziri_nkhaleb.self_image: Bridge between Gallaecian and Imazuran, keeper of husband's legacy, protector of her caravan family
taziri_nkhaleb.recreation: Singing road songs at crossroads, updating ledgers by candlelight, polishing husband's waystone marker
taziri_nkhaleb.dreams: Davin becomes respected Ilmenor scholar, safe profitable routes forever, finding who killed Irven
anecdotes: Once outbid a hobgoblin warlord for iron nails using only dried figs and honey
Navigated through fey-glamoured woods by singing Isarna hymns until dawn
Refused Covenant-Bound 'special cargo' offer despite massive payment
tragedies: Husband Irven killed in southern swamps 8 years ago (believes lizard-men responsible)
Has hidden map of 'safe paths' Irven drew before death—guards it obsessively
Receives coded letters from Davin hinting at dangerous Ilmenor secrets
taziri_nkhaleb.pc_leverage: Prove lizard-men didn't kill Irven OR find actual killers; protect Davin; share intelligence on humanoid coordination; respect her authority on the road

caravan.route: Caelrun to Tagmira (primary), broader Luric Marches circuit
caravan.current_leg: Caelrun to Tagmira — party traveling with her
caravan.brother_azghar: Teamster, Taziri's brother, manages the wagons day-to-day
caravan.waystone_observance: Will stop at Waystone of Isarna west of Tagmira for offering (bread, silver thread)
caravan.dm_notes: Taziri is the party's most reliable civilian connection across the Luric Marches. She knows both Caelrun and Tagmira well and can brief the party on surface-level politics of either. She has no knowledge of the undead alliance. Her Isarna observance at the waystone is a natural scene transition into Tagmira.

mother_yaleth.home: Nemetra—has never left, refuses to, sees leaving as betrayal of the sick and dying who need her
mother_yaleth.faction: Herb-wives (unofficial leader), allied with House Yebrin
mother_yaleth.appearance: Late 60s, weathered face with deep lines, sharp grey eyes that miss nothing, faded green shawl, earth-stained hands, walks with carved hazelwood staff, wears dried herb bundles at belt
mother_yaleth.wants: Truth about northern woods threat, village to take danger seriously, protect the younger herb-wives
mother_yaleth.does_not_want: Militia interference, fools dismissing her warnings, goblins harmed before she learns what they know
mother_yaleth.oddly_also: Heavy smoker (pipe, strong tobacco) despite it damaging her lungs—considered unfeminine, she doesn't care
mother_yaleth.thinks_with: Observation, herb-lore passed down generations, pattern recognition from decades of births and deaths
mother_yaleth.family: House Yebrin (distant cousin), 6 herb-wives under her guidance, no living children
mother_yaleth.self_image: Proud of her humility, quietly smug about being right when others are wrong
mother_yaleth.recreation: Smoking her pipe on the compound steps at dusk, watching village life
mother_yaleth.dreams: Establish Nemetra as tobacco exporter, prove herb-wives' wisdom to the fools, die respected
anecdotes: Delivered Captain Durvin himself during a winter storm—he was blue when he came out, she breathed life back into him. He's never thanked her once.
Diagnosed House Halbet's pottery master with lung rot from kiln smoke 20 years ago—he ignored her, kept working, died within the year. His son listens now.
Once talked down a delirious wounded orc at the forest edge using only hand signs and offering him water. Militia wanted to kill it. She sent it limping north with bandaged leg.
tragedies: Lost all three of her children (two daughters, one son) to fever in a single week during Year 285. She knows every herb that might have saved them—none grew close enough in time.
Her husband left her 40 years ago, said she 'loved plants more than people.' He remarried in Tagmira. She never speaks of him but keeps his tobacco pouch.

Her mentor, Old Kessa, was killed by militiamen who thought she was consorting with fey (she was gathering moonlit herbs). Yaleth found the body. Durvin's father led that patrol—she's never forgotten.
mother_yaleth.pc_leverage: Bring her proof of northern woods threat, protect goblins from militia, share intelligence she can't gather herself, respect her knowledge

caelrun.name: Caelrun
caelrun.character: Humid, productive, quietly watched by forces villagers don't acknowledge

atmosphere.mood: Industrious, practical, prosperity with undercurrent of carefully ignored truths

tensions: Aurka activity drawing unwanted attention to ruins
Unspoken lizard-man control - acknowledged by few, ignored by most
Fringe workers (leech gatherers, bog-edge camps) operate outside village authority
rumors: Aurka found carved stones in submerged temple foundations
Nebruz smugglers use secret lake channels at night
Old temple columns visible in shallows during summer droughts

defenses.militia: Ceremonial, mostly for disputes and drunk fishers
defenses.reality: Lizard-men enforce territorial boundaries from shadows
defenses.village_understanding: Most residents believe their prosperity comes from hard work and good location, unaware of the arrangement

caelrun.trade_focus: Food surplus provider - feeds other Marches villages
caelrun.road_position: On main Lamares-Orziben caravan route, between Nemetra and Tagmira
caelrun.hidden_truth: Village exists by lizard-man sufferance, most residents unaware of this arrangement. A few families know and maintain the unspoken compact.

nebruz.name: Nebruz
nebruz.primary_activity: Smuggling, black market trade, hidden waystation
nebruz.character: Hidden, opportunistic, dangerous - exists in shadows both literal and political

salmura.name: Salmura
salmura.primary_activity: Fish drying and smoking, salt fish production
salmura.character: Windswept, isolated, hardworking - smoke always rising from drying racks

carvoal.name: Carvoal
carvoal.primary_activity: Charcoal burning, timber cutting at swamp edge
carvoal.character: Smoky, watchful, tense - closest to lizard-man patrols, sees things others don't

xuncal.name: Xuncal
xuncal.primary_activity: Reed cutting, rush weaving, basket making
xuncal.character: Quiet, rhythmic, traditional - families have worked these beds for generations

angueira.name: Angueira
angueira.character: Secretive, superstitious, skilled - guard eel-trap techniques jealously

ordun_tarnes.appearance: Weathered, patient, knows soil and seasons
ordun_tarnes.wants: Good harvests, peace with all parties including lizard-men
ordun_tarnes.does_not_want: Village discovering his quiet negotiations, conflict disrupting planting seasons
ordun_tarnes.thinks_with: Seasonal cycles, careful observation, pragmatic compromise
ordun_tarnes.family: House Tarnes patriarch, extended family of 15+
ordun_tarnes.self_image: Keeper of ancient compact, protector through diplomacy not strength
ordun_tarnes.recreation: Walking barley fields at dawn alone
ordun_tarnes.dreams: Pass knowledge of lizard-man arrangement to worthy successor
ordun_tarnes.pc_leverage: Respect for the compact, help with harvest, keep his secret

merra_of_the_fields.wants: Efficient land use, respect for agricultural expertise
merra_of_the_fields.does_not_want: Fishers' opinions on farming, wasted resources
merra_of_the_fields.family: Married to Tarnes cousin, three children work the fields
merra_of_the_fields.self_image: The one who actually makes things run
merra_of_the_fields.recreation: Doesn't have time for recreation, resents the question
merra_of_the_fields.dreams: Expand barley cultivation, prove women can manage large operations
merra_of_the_fields.pc_leverage: Practical help, respect for her authority, efficient solutions

kelvan_bresul.appearance: Soft around the middle, drinks too much, ceremonial armor kept polished
kelvan_bresul.wants: To be taken seriously, to not see what he half-sees in marshes
kelvan_bresul.does_not_want: Real conflict, questions about militia effectiveness, night patrols near swamp edge
kelvan_bresul.family: House Bresul, wife tired of his drinking, two teenage sons
kelvan_bresul.self_image: Protector of Caelrun (deeply insecure about this)
kelvan_bresul.recreation: Drinking at the Waterside Tavern, telling war stories from his youth that may not be true
kelvan_bresul.dreams: Prove himself in real conflict (terrified this might actually happen)
kelvan_bresul.pc_leverage: Validate his importance, bring him information that makes him look competent, don't expose his fear

sarith_the_scarred.faction: Former Elunad, adopted House Hallan, village elder
sarith_the_scarred.appearance: Old wounds visible, walks with cane, sharp eyes despite age
sarith_the_scarred.wants: Maintain lizard-man compact, village safety, peaceful succession of knowledge
sarith_the_scarred.does_not_want: Foolish heroes disrupting arrangement, Aurka attracting wrong attention
sarith_the_scarred.thinks_with: Decades of border wisdom, Elunad training, cold pragmatism
sarith_the_scarred.family: Adopted by Hallan after serving with them, never married, mentors quietly
sarith_the_scarred.self_image: Last guardian of a necessary truth
sarith_the_scarred.recreation: Carving waystones, teaching those few who listen
sarith_the_scarred.dreams: Die knowing compact will hold after he's gone
sarith_the_scarred.pc_leverage: Prove trustworthiness, demonstrate understanding of complex situations, Elunad connection

mother_hildreth.appearance: Elderly, serene, grey wool robes, moves slowly but purposefully
mother_hildreth.wants: Proper funeral rites, peace for the dying, respect for Thulda's mysteries
mother_hildreth.does_not_want: Rushed burials, impiety, questions about death she cannot answer
mother_hildreth.family: House Velmari, outlived husband and two children
mother_hildreth.self_image: Shepherd of souls, keeper of the final threshold
mother_hildreth.recreation: Spinning wool by the shrine, humming old funeral songs
mother_hildreth.dreams: Train successor, die in her sleep at the shrine
mother_hildreth.pc_leverage: Respect for rituals, help with funeral preparations, listening to her wisdom

tovar_the_boatwright.appearance: Perfectionist, calloused hands, sawdust in beard, critical eye
tovar_the_boatwright.wants: Every boat built to last decades, apprentices who listen, proper materials
tovar_the_boatwright.does_not_want: Rush jobs, cheap timber, people who don't understand craftsmanship
tovar_the_boatwright.thinks_with: Wood grain, water flow, structural integrity
tovar_the_boatwright.family: House Bresul, three apprentices (terrorized), wife understands his moods
tovar_the_boatwright.self_image: Last true craftsman in a world of shortcuts
tovar_the_boatwright.recreation: There is no recreation, only boat work
tovar_the_boatwright.dreams: Build the perfect fishing boat, retire without regrets
tovar_the_boatwright.pc_leverage: Respect his craft, patience, bring quality materials, don't rush him

yavra_quickwater.faction: House Norvak (Caelrun branch), ferry operator based in Nebruz
yavra_quickwater.appearance: Amazigh woman, agile, knows every channel, suspicious eyes
yavra_quickwater.wants: Profitable smuggling routes, Nebruz connections maintained, not getting caught
yavra_quickwater.does_not_want: Questions about nighttime ferry trips, militia attention, loose-lipped passengers
yavra_quickwater.thinks_with: Hidden channels, tide timing, who can be trusted
yavra_quickwater.family: House Norvak, but operates independently, sends money home
yavra_quickwater.recreation: Gambling with Nebruz smugglers, winning usually
yavra_quickwater.dreams: Retire wealthy to Imazuran, never explain where money came from
yavra_quickwater.pc_leverage: Gold, discretion, Nebruz connections, don't threaten her operation

brecht_hallan.appearance: Commands 40+ boats, hard but fair, weathered from decades on water
brecht_hallan.wants: Productive fishing, family prosperity, compact honored quietly
brecht_hallan.does_not_want: Outsiders disrupting arrangement, family members talking too much, bad fishing seasons
brecht_hallan.thinks_with: Fish migrations, weather signs, political calculation
brecht_hallan.family: House Hallan patriarch, 40+ extended family, inherited secret from father
brecht_hallan.self_image: Steward of village prosperity through necessary silence
brecht_hallan.dreams: Pass fleet and secret to worthy heir, die on the water
brecht_hallan.pc_leverage: Prove discretion, help fishing operations, understand burden of secrets

essa_the_net_mender.appearance: Blind in one eye from fishing accident, hands never stop moving, weathered face
essa_the_net_mender.wants: Guild neutrality maintained, quality rope production, gossip (currency of information)
essa_the_net_mender.does_not_want: Family feuds disrupting guild, shoddy work, being left out of news
essa_the_net_mender.thinks_with: Rope tension, social networks, who owes whom
essa_the_net_mender.family: House Caernach, widowed, grown children fish for other families
essa_the_net_mender.self_image: Spider at center of web, keeper of village knowledge
essa_the_net_mender.recreation: Gossip is recreation, knitting while listening
essa_the_net_mender.dreams: Retire as most respected elder, write nothing down
essa_the_net_mender.pc_leverage: Trade information, respect guild neutrality, commission quality rope

brother_kellis.appearance: Lives in reed hut, marks safe water paths with cairns, speaks in riddles
brother_kellis.wants: Safe crossings for travelers, communion with water spirits, solitude mostly
brother_kellis.does_not_want: Village politics, questions about their past, cairns disturbed
brother_kellis.thinks_with: Water currents, liminal spaces, Ossamar's mysteries
brother_kellis.family: No family ties, left former life behind decades ago
brother_kellis.self_image: Between-dweller, neither shore nor water, guide for the crossing
brother_kellis.recreation: Wading at dawn, arranging stones, watching reflections
brother_kellis.dreams: Perfect understanding of Ossamar's nature, death by drowning peacefully
brother_kellis.pc_leverage: Respect for Ossamar, offerings at cairns, understanding of liminality

penna.appearance: Middle-aged, efficient, knows compound routines better than family members
penna.wants: Smooth household operation, respect for her knowledge, security for her children
penna.does_not_want: Chaos, disrespect, her children treated as lesser
penna.family: Servant family for three generations, husband also serves Hallan, four children
penna.self_image: Backbone of great house, underestimated and essential
penna.recreation: Teaching daughters household management, rare moments of stillness
penna.dreams: Eldest daughter marries above station, family rises
penna.pc_leverage: Respect for her position, help her children, recognize her competence

dren.appearance: Young, bitter, stained from fish oil rendering, thin from hard work
dren.wants: Freedom from debt, revenge on Norvak, escape from Caelrun
dren.does_not_want: Seven more years of service, condescension, being reminded of debt
dren.family: Father took debt, died, debt passed to son, siblings scattered
dren.recreation: Drinking stolen wine, fantasizing about burning oil pits
dren.dreams: Flee to Velgroth, never see fish again, Norvak suffers somehow
dren.pc_leverage: Help reduce debt, offer escape route, validate his anger, employment after freedom

kessa_the_leech_wife.appearance: Stained fingers, knows medicinal herbs, lives alone on marsh edge
kessa_the_leech_wife.wants: Left alone, herbs to grow wild, occasional trade for necessities
kessa_the_leech_wife.does_not_want: Village interference, questions about what she sees, herb-gathering competition
kessa_the_leech_wife.family: No ties, may have fled something in past
kessa_the_leech_wife.self_image: Useful outcast, too valuable to exile, too strange to integrate
kessa_the_leech_wife.recreation: Observing marsh life, talking to leeches
kessa_the_leech_wife.dreams: Die in marsh, body returned to water and mud
kessa_the_leech_wife.pc_leverage: Respect her knowledge, trade fairly, don't pry into past, leave her alone mostly

varren_muddfoot.appearance: Nervous energy, lives in stilted shack, stinks of frog and marsh
varren_muddfoot.wants: Aurka payment, information to trade, respect from family (won't admit this)
varren_muddfoot.does_not_want: Family reconciliation attempts, being forced to choose sides, caught informing
varren_muddfoot.thinks_with: Information as currency, survival at margins, opportunism
varren_muddfoot.family: Distant Bresul cousin, disowned for theft (claims he was framed)
varren_muddfoot.self_image: Survivor, wronged but unbowed, eventually they'll see
varren_muddfoot.recreation: Spying on ruins, catching frogs, trading rumors
varren_muddfoot.dreams: Aurka find something valuable, he gets cut, family begs forgiveness he'll deny
varren_muddfoot.pc_leverage: Information trade, gold, Aurka connections, don't threaten his operation

submerged_temple_garden_enki_ki.name: The Submerged Temple-Garden of Enki and Ki
submerged_temple_garden_enki_ki.location: Northern depths of Caelrun lake, 40-60 feet below surface

overview.what_it_is: Massive pre-Silence temple complex with two wings: Enki's Archive (stone halls, bronze scrolls, agricultural/medical texts) and Ki's Vault (ceramic seed storage with preservation enchantments still active). Flooded terraces where enchanted plants still grow underwater, twisted and strange.
overview.why_it_exists: Built as combined library and seed bank - knowledge preservation and agricultural insurance for civilization. Dedicated to Enki (god of water, wisdom, civilization) and Ki (earth mother, fertility).
overview.current_state: Submerged, partially collapsed, overtaken by mutated magical plant growth. Preservation enchantments failing but still functional in places. Something still moves in the halls.
overview.historical_context: 300 years underwater since the Silence. Lizard-men know of it, permit limited human exploration as part of Caelrun compact. Aurka have been diving it for years with mixed success.

physical: 40-60 foot depth - fatal for normal diving
magical: Failing preservation enchantments create unpredictable effects
Memory silt releases psychic echoes of the Silence
Some plants animated by degrading magic
political: Northern waters deeper into lizard-man territory
Full exploitation crosses unstated compact line
Rival Aurka groups competing for finds
Village won't support major expeditions (compact violation)
environmental: Storms churn silt, make diving impossible for days
access_challenges.temporal: Aurka report activity increasing - brighter glow, more movement, fish avoiding area. Something may be waking.

creatures: The Pale Carp - albino fish, too many teeth, swarm wounded divers
Something that moves through halls with purpose
magical_hazards: Lotus-Breath Kelp - spore release causes euphoric visions, divers forget to surface
Memory Silt - psychic echoes of the Silence when disturbed
Black vines (unknown effect, Aurka refuse to discuss)
Transformation - one diver came up changed, speaks only in agricultural metaphors
unknown_threats: Western wing - Aurka stopped diving there, won't explain why
Bodies never recovered from certain sections
The green-blue glow on moonless nights

treasure: Worked bronze tools and ceremonial items
knowledge: Information about what caused the Silence (possibly)
magical_items: Ceramic seed jars with active preservation enchantments
Extinct crop seeds (blight-resistant grain, year-round fruit trees, lost medicinal plants)
Possibly functioning Old God ritual components
strategic_value: Knowledge valuable to Orziben alchemists, Imazuran botanists, noble houses
Understanding of Old God magic systems

aurka.goals: Recover seed vault contents, sell texts to highest bidders, establish controlled access
aurka.resources_committed: Multiple diving expeditions, Varren Muddfoot as local recruiter, mapping efforts
aurka.knowledge_level: High - have mapped portions, know dangers, found seed jar, keeping vault location secret

lizard_men.goals: Maintain territorial control, enforce compact limits, watch human foolishness
lizard_men.resources_committed: Passive observation, will not rescue failed divers
lizard_men.knowledge_level: Complete - navigate ruins easily, know what's dangerous, find human obsession with 'plant-jars and scratched stones' amusing

ilmenor.goals: Recover pre-Silence arcane texts, study failing enchantments, control dangerous knowledge
ilmenor.resources_committed: Agents may buy Aurka finds, no direct expeditions yet
ilmenor.knowledge_level: Low - know ruins exist, suspect Old God magic present, want access to texts

caelrun_village.goals: Maintain compact, benefit from Aurka spending, avoid angering lizard-men
caelrun_village.knowledge_level: Medium - elders (Brecht, Sarith, Ordun) know compact details, general population knows 'dangerous old temple, people die, not our business'

common_rumors: Old temple in deep north water, very dangerous
Aurka dive it for relics, several have died
Don't fish directly over ruins (bad luck, plants tangle nets)
If you see green glow, row away
Fisher decades ago opened a jar, planted seeds - whole family died of fever

varren_muddfoot.cost_to_acquire: Few silver, drinks, or information trade
information: Temple is there, Aurka diving it
Something down there still works (the glow)

actual_aurka_diver.cost_to_acquire: Expensive (50+ gp) or significant favor, rare access
information: Best diving: late summer, warmest water, best visibility
Never dive alone, never at night, never touch black vines

sarith_the_scarred.cost_to_acquire: Trust, Elunad connection, or proving competence

forbidden_knowledge: What Aurka saw in western wing (won't sell at any price)
Exact seed vault location (Aurka keeping secret)
Why bodies aren't recovered from certain sections

mandatory: Water breathing (potion, spell, magic item) - 40-60 feet for extended time
highly_recommended: Freedom of movement or similar (plant entanglement)
Navigation method (easy to get lost in silt)
useful: Read Languages or Old Tongue knowledge
Lizard-man goodwill (won't help but won't hinder)

entrance_colonnade.name: The Entrance Colonnade
entrance_colonnade.description: Massive columns barely visible from surface. Four still standing, three collapsed. Wide steps descend deeper. Plant growth thickest here - kelp forests and vine tangles. Pale carp school nearby.
connections: Steps lead to Archive Hall (east) and Garden Terraces (west)

archive_hall_enki.name: Enki's Archive Hall
archive_hall_enki.description: Long stone chamber, shelves carved into walls. Many bronze scroll cases remain, some sealed, some breached. Silt thick on floor. Eastern wall partially collapsed. Faint inscriptions in Old Tongue. Cold current flows through.
treasures: Bronze scroll cases (agricultural, medical, irrigation texts)

seed_vault_ki.name: Ki's Seed Vault
seed_vault_ki.description: Circular chamber, ceramic jars in wall niches (200+). Many shattered, some intact with glowing preservation runes. Center altar has growth diagram inscriptions. Western wall shows murals of gardens. Aurka have mapped this but keep location secret.
dangers: Aurka guards/traps if they've secured it

garden_terraces.name: The Flooded Garden Terraces
garden_terraces.description: Stepped terraces, once open to sky, now deep underwater. Magical plants grow wild - some beautiful, some horrifying. Bioluminescent flowers, carnivorous vines, kelp that whispers. Original purpose: cultivate sacred plants. Current state: twisted magical ecosystem.
dangers: Easy to get lost in growth

western_wing.name: The Western Wing (Unexplored)
western_wing.description: Aurka stopped diving here. Entrance visible but they won't approach. Green-blue glow strongest from this direction. Voices underwater seem to originate here. Architecture different - older? Disturbing carvings visible on entrance columns.
dangers: Lights that move with purpose originate here
Bodies not recovered from this section
Changed diver was exploring here before transformation
treasures: Possibly something that should stay buried

success: Seed jars recovered - agricultural revolution possible
Texts recovered - knowledge boom, Ilmenor interest
Potential ongoing revenue from controlled access
Understanding of Old God preservation magic
Caelrun elders grateful if compact honored
partial_success: Some treasures recovered, but party member transformed/cursed
Knowledge gained but rivals also got valuable intel
Structural damage causes sections to collapse permanently
Awakened something in the Western Wing
failure: Party member(s) drowned, bodies not recovered
Village shrugs - 'another group tried the ruins'
discovery: If Western Wing explored - major plot revelations possible
If preservation enchantments fully studied - arcane breakthrough
If all seed jars catalogued - complete pre-Silence botanical knowledge
If voices understood - possibly communication with Old God remnants/undead
If Memory Silt properly investigated - witness the Silence firsthand (dangerous)

dm_notes.design_intent: Multi-session underwater dungeon crawl with resource management (air, light, time). Rewards exploration and preparation over brute force. Ties to setting cosmology (Old Gods, the Silence) while remaining accessible to mid-level party. Creates ongoing campaign resource (controlled access to ruins).
scaling_options: Level 2-3: Entrance and Archive Hall only, Aurka-guided, simple recovery mission
Level 3-5: Full complex except Western Wing, competing with Aurka, multiple treasures
Level 5-7: Western Wing exploration, awakened guardians, cosmic horror elements
Adjust creature numbers, add/remove magical hazards, change Aurka from allies to rivals
alternative_approaches: Ally with Aurka - guided expedition, split rewards
Rival Aurka - race to seed vault, potential combat underwater
Negotiate lizard-men permission for major expedition (difficult, interesting RP)
Magical approach - water breathing + water walking for surface approach, teleportation
Research first - buy all available info, prepare perfectly, single efficient dive
Ignore diving - hire/coerce Aurka to retrieve specific items
Political - convince Caelrun elders to support, change compact terms
integration_points: Seeds recovered could become trade goods, political leverage
Texts could advance PC knowledge of Old Gods, foreshadow Corpse Planes
Changed diver could become recurring NPC - prophet/oracle/problem
Western Wing could connect to larger Old God plot
Lizard-men relationship established here affects future swamp adventures
Success attracts Ilmenor attention - recruitment or problems
Failure creates reputation consequences in Caelrun
Discovery in Memory Silt could reveal Silence details relevant to main plot

tagmira.name: Tagmira
tagmira.character: A hardscrabble upland village hemmed by forest and old fears. Houses cluster around a central hearth-hall. The people are clannish, proud of small dignities, and slow to trust outsiders — but Sardet's deeds at the Circle of Cossus and the party's role fighting the orc raid have bought real goodwill.

sounds: dogs that go quiet near the forest edge at dusk
smells: something faintly earthy and wrong near the Bramblewood
atmosphere.mood: Watchful but not unfriendly. The village has suffered orc pressure, lost Old Maedra without explanation, and carries a low hum of unease it cannot name.

gravefield_of_thulda.description: Burial mounds northeast of the village, encircled by carved posts. Wool wrappings and grave-goods mark each mound. Tended by two quiet widows. Site of Ildun-Ayenar's burial — the cleric of Ossamar the undead sacrificed to keep the Sphere running. His grave is unmarked by any symbol of Ossamar; the votive stone found in his hand bore old-empire symbols, not of his god. Some villagers visit and feel uneasy without knowing why.
gravefield_of_thulda.current_state: Active. The widows who tend it have noted the grass grows oddly thick over Ildun's mound.
gravefield_of_thulda.dm_notes: The party knows the undead sacrificed Ildun here. If they return and examine the grave carefully, faint necrotic resonance is detectable. The widows will mention that no one from Ossamar's order has come to claim his memory.

ford_of_ossamar.description: A wide clear ford three miles east, marked by cairns and iron braziers. Once tended by Ildun-Ayenar. The shrine is now untended — braziers cold, cairns crumbling slightly at the edges. Travelers still stop here but find it comfortless.
ford_of_ossamar.current_state: Abandoned since Ildun's disappearance. The undead took him here. The ford is still usable but the sacred atmosphere has drained away.
ford_of_ossamar.dm_notes: The party forgave the undead for this sacrifice. Villagers just think Ildun wandered off or died quietly. Anyone who knew him well — Maedra did — carries the truth into undeath.

circle_of_cossus.description: A stone ring in a forest glade south of the village. Originally a fey circle corrupted ~150 years ago when men tried to sacralize it, now consecrated to Cossus as a trial-by-combat ground. Sardet officiated the duel between Tenn Darnel and Ulan Caernach here, which won the party real standing in the village.
circle_of_cossus.current_state: The stones still hum faintly when oaths are spoken. The fey corruption is old and dormant but not gone.
circle_of_cossus.dm_notes: Sardet's officiating here is remembered. If the party needs to call in goodwill with Tagmira, this is a touchstone. The residual fey taint is undetectable except to a druid (Brigantios could sense it). It is old and dormant but not gone.

mound_of_threads.description: A rounded earthen mound on the western ridge, shrine to Thulda sacred to shepherds and mourners. Wool offerings are laid here; it serves as both tomb and temple. Still actively maintained.
mound_of_threads.current_state: Active. Wool offerings accumulate. The Elaran family tends it most faithfully.
mound_of_threads.dm_notes: No supernatural activity here yet, but Thulda's domain is death and mourning — thematically resonant with everything happening at the temple.

tree_of_mist_song.description: A lightning-scarred ash north of the village atop a stony knoll. One of the oldest trees in the region. Stones circle its roots, carved in spirals and claw marks. A dryad is trapped within — bitterly lonely, her forest long since cleared except for this one isolated tree. The party has not yet managed to rouse her.
tree_of_mist_song.current_state: The dryad is present but dormant from loneliness and despair. She will respond to genuine druidic attention or to someone who sits with her tree in true silence for a full turn.
tree_of_mist_song.dm_notes: Once roused she will be desperate and raw — centuries of isolation. She knows things about the Bramblewood's origins (it was seeded by the old gods as a perimeter). Could become an ally or a tragedy depending on the party's choices. Nabia, Bracara's mentor, once held vigil here.

waystone_of_isarna.description: A stone marker etched with a crescent and hoofprint west of Tagmira, one of Isarna's oldest way-shrines. Taziri's caravan will almost certainly stop here for an offering on arrival. Bread and silver thread are traditional.
waystone_of_isarna.current_state: Active and well-maintained by passing travelers.
waystone_of_isarna.dm_notes: Good moment for roleplay as the caravan arrives. Taziri's offering here is a natural transition into Tagmira proper.

hearth_hall.description: The central gathering hall of Tagmira. Food, drink, and village business. Militia captain Hadran Throsk holds informal court here most evenings.

bramblewood.description: The dense thorn-forest north of Tagmira. Grown by the old gods as a living perimeter, seeded with divine fear and watered by clerics' blood (see Thorn-Bound Covenant). Contains the Temple of the Old Gods. The undead cannot stray far from it without losing consciousness unless they pay a high magical cost.
bramblewood.current_state: The orc Bronze Host is pressing from the northwest. The undead are aware but cannot project force outside the Bramblewood. The party is caught between these pressures.
bramblewood.dm_notes: See temple and orc sections below.

factions.name: Village Militia
factions.strength: 10 active, 6 veterans who can be called
factions.awareness_of_undead: Dim — they know something is wrong in the Bramblewood, have heard stories, but have no clear picture
factions.awareness_of_orc_threat: High — the Bronze Host has been pushing hunters out of the northwest grounds for two months
factions.disposition_to_party: Favorable — the party fought alongside them against the orc raid
factions.character: Not a garrison force — Tagmirans are tough and have always spent the spring patrolling and intimidating the orcs. They know what war costs. The problem is not their courage but that the Bronze Host has suddenly become something different: better equipped, coordinated, and backed by something the militia does not understand.
factions.spring_patrol_tradition: Every spring the militia patrols the northwest aggressively to keep orc pressure down. This has worked for a generation. It is not working now.

factions.name: House Elders (informal council)
factions.awareness_of_undead: None formally — Maedra has 'disappeared', no uproar
factions.awareness_of_orc_threat: Moderate — they know hunters are being pushed back, do not yet grasp the scale
factions.disposition_to_party: Mixed but leaning favorable after Sardet's officiating and the orc fight

factions.name: Temple of the Old Gods (Zimru's undead)
factions.current_posture: Quiet and patient. Sphere is functional after the orc sacrifice. Want more sacrifices — specifically more orcs, which conveniently aligns with Tagmira's orc problem. Also quietly seeking Tagmiran 'recruits' for undeath. Maedra is now Zimru's consort and seen by the undead as nearly a prophet.
factions.disposition_to_party: Allied, with expectations. Zimru will want an accounting of the sacrifice and will press for the next one.

factions.name: Bronze Host (Pact-Render orcs)
factions.location: Northwest hunting grounds and the Bronzeworking Triangle beyond
factions.current_posture: Actively pushing Tagmirans out of the northwest forest. Two months since the raid — all named orc leaders survived, equipment upgraded via Pact-Render fey bronze. Making a play for the hunting grounds as first step toward assault on Tagmira proper. The undead know and cannot act.

tensions: Maedra has vanished — whispers but no answers. The undead want more Tagmiran recruits.

The Bronze Host has seized the northwest hunting grounds. This is not a rumor — hunters have been driven back, one was killed, and Tagmira will face serious food pressure this winter if nothing changes. Hadran and the elders know it.

The orcs are suddenly too powerful. They have bronze weapons, coordination, and something behind them. The Tagmirans are tough and have always held the line in spring patrols — but this is different, and it is starting to dawn on them.

Two Bronze Host orcs were brought to Tagmira as captives to witness the trial by combat — meant as intimidation. It may have given the orcs useful intelligence about Tagmira's current strength instead.
The undead alliance is secret. If Hadran or the elders learned the party is allied with the Bramblewood undead, there would be a reckoning.
House Darnel and House Caernach water-rights tension persists after the duel — Tenn Darnel lost and has not forgiven it.
Ulan Caernach and Rondasek Elaran are back to village life but changed — they've seen skeletons fight and orcs bleed. They are quietly restless.
rumors: Old Maedra walked into the Bramblewood one night and didn't come back. Some say she went to die in peace. Others say the wood took her.
Borric of House Caernach swears he saw Maedra on the Bramblewood path at dusk — but she looked wrong. He won't say how.
The Ford of Ossamar has gone cold since Ildun died. Offerings left there feel unaccepted.

defenses.veterans: 6 callable, including Borric and Feln of House Caernach
defenses.walls: Wooden palisade wall surrounding the village
defenses.notes: Tagmira is not fortified. A serious Bronze Host assault would be catastrophic without party intervention or some other force.

tagmira.trade_focus: Taziri's caravan is the primary outside trade connection
tagmira.road_position: End of a trade spur; Taziri's route terminates here

hadran_throsk.appearance: Scarred veteran, limp from an old wound, booming voice that carries across the hall. Middle-aged, weathered, still formidable. Wears his militia captain's insignia like a priest wears vestments.
hadran_throsk.and_yet: Carves delicate wood figurines of ancient war heroes in his off hours — small, patient work that surprises anyone who sees it.
hadran_throsk.wants: To protect Tagmira without spending lives he cannot replace. Wants to understand the orc threat well enough to do something about it.
hadran_throsk.does_not_want: Outside interference that makes the village dependent. Another generation bled out in a fight they didn't start.
hadran_throsk.oddly_also: Wants Tagmira to become fortified enough that it won't need him specifically.
hadran_throsk.thinks_with: Hard experience. Deeply skeptical of magic but respects the gods. Distrusts anything he can't see clearly.
hadran_throsk.family: Estranged daughter in Velgroth-Lucernia. No surviving kin in the village.
hadran_throsk.self_image: The last responsible man in a village full of people who haven't yet learned what war costs.
hadran_throsk.dreams: A fortified Tagmira that doesn't need him.
anecdotes: Once rallied the militia during a night raid five winters ago — that's why they follow him.
Served as a mercenary for the Confederation before returning home.
tragedies: Estrangement from his daughter, reasons unstated.
hadran_throsk.pc_leverage: He respects those who fight well and act with restraint. The party earned credit in the orc raid. If they bring him actionable intelligence about the Bronze Host, he will act on it. He does not yet know about the undead alliance — that knowledge would complicate everything.
hadran_throsk.dm_notes: Hadran is primarily a civic/political resource. The party will likely need to decide how much to tell him about the Bronze Host's Pact-Render backing, and whether to ever reveal the undead alliance. He is not the kind of man who would be comfortable with the latter.

ulan_caernach.appearance: 22, lean and deliberate in movement. Carries the curved knife Mellan gave him. Won the duel against Tenn Darnel at the Circle of Cossus. Has since fought orcs and skeletons alongside the party.
ulan_caernach.and_yet: Rubs his thumb across his knife's carved grip when thinking — a tell he's unaware of.
ulan_caernach.wants: To understand what he's become after the things he's seen. Quietly proud of the duel win; privately unsure what it means.
ulan_caernach.does_not_want: To be someone who goes looking for violence. But he would answer a call.
ulan_caernach.oddly_also: Wants someone to tell him whether what the party is doing — the undead, all of it — is right or wrong. He suspects no one will.
ulan_caernach.thinks_with: Patience and observation. Tactical rather than impulsive.
ulan_caernach.family: Mother Rinna (manages Caernach hall), brother Theren (wants to join the Elunad), stepfather Mellan (hunter).
ulan_caernach.self_image: A hunter's son who won a duel and then fought things that should not exist. Still processing.
ulan_caernach.recreation: Hunting. Sitting with Tussen and the older fighters in the evenings.
ulan_caernach.dreams: Vaguely: that the things he's seen don't come to Tagmira's door.
anecdotes: Won the Trial by Combat with Tenn Darnel — Sardet officiated.
Fought orcs and animated skeletons with the party.
ulan_caernach.pc_leverage: Fought alongside the party. Will answer a call without much persuasion. Sardet in particular has his respect.

rondasek_elaran.appearance: Young fighter, Elaran coloring — dark-browed, stocky. Less polished than Ulan, more openly eager.
rondasek_elaran.and_yet: Has started leaving small offerings at the Tree of Mist-Song since fighting the skeletons. Won't say why.
rondasek_elaran.wants: To be someone the village talks about the way they talk about Hadran.
rondasek_elaran.does_not_want: To be ordinary after what he's seen.
rondasek_elaran.oddly_also: Is quietly fascinated by the undead in a way he'd never admit aloud.
rondasek_elaran.family: House Elaran — under Dornan's authority, cousin to the ridge-courier line.
rondasek_elaran.self_image: Someone who has been tested and did not break.
rondasek_elaran.recreation: Sparring. Hunting with Ulan when Ulan will have him.
anecdotes: Fought alongside the party against orcs and skeletons.
rondasek_elaran.pc_leverage: Eager to be called again. Will follow without much prompting. The party should be careful — his courage outruns his experience.

zimru.home: Temple of the Old Gods, Bramblewood
zimru.appearance: Tall, stooped slightly as though time itself bends around him. Skin like oiled parchment drawn thin over bone. Gold-threaded vestments long dulled but impeccably arranged. Eyes that burn with focused discipline — he remembers sight and continues the act out of sheer will.
zimru.and_yet: Occasionally addresses the party in the plural as though speaking to an assembly, then catches himself — a remnant of addressing full congregations three centuries gone.
zimru.wants: Order. The preservation of the old laws in the gods' absence. To prevent humanity's extermination by guiding what remains. To continue the necromantic plan with Maedra. More sacrifices to keep the Sphere running.
zimru.does_not_want: Forgetting — of names, rites, obligations. The chaos of mortal improvisation. Self-interested necromancy that undermines the temple's order.
zimru.oddly_also: Wants the party to understand that he salutes them for choosing action over purity — the undead they destroyed 'would have let the world die praying.'
zimru.thinks_with: Legal codes. Every decision is framed as judgment, not preference. Chaos disgusts him; even cruel law commands his respect.
zimru.family: None living. The undead of the temple are his order.
zimru.self_image: The last functioning priest of a dead divine system. Not a monster — a caretaker. The gods failed; their law must not.
zimru.recreation: The Sphere. Observation. Issuing decrees to an empty hall.
zimru.dreams: A world ordered by the old laws, maintained through undeath until the gods return — or until he accepts they will not.
anecdotes: 'If the gods have died, then their law must rule in their stead.'
'Those of my people whom you destroyed clung to purity. They would have let the world die praying. You chose. For this, I salute you.'

'I can keep watch through the Sphere. I can guide what remains. Maedra's change will anchor life beyond death. Endorse this, and you will act with sight. Refuse, and you will fight blind while the last light gutters.'
tragedies: Three centuries of perfect lucidity watching everything he served collapse and be forgotten.
zimru.pc_leverage: He respects the party for their choices and will treat them as functioning agents — not mortals to be toyed with. Approach him formally. He responds to order, competence, and those who honor obligations. He will press them on the next sacrifice. He knows about the orc threat via the Sphere and cannot act on it himself — this is leverage the party holds.

mechanics.special: Level drain on touch — uses it as judgment, not rage

zimru.dm_notes: Voice and demeanor: undead Tywin Lannister. Never rush his dialogue. No contractions. Every line sounds like a decree being read aloud. He will assess the party's order, not their morality.

maedra.home: Temple of the Old Gods, Bramblewood
maedra.appearance: What she looked like in life: a small, ancient woman who appeared at funerals and vigils without being seen to arrive. What she looks like now: similar, but the stillness is absolute. The undead of the temple regard her with something close to reverence.
maedra.and_yet: She wept at Ildun-Ayenar's burial — before her transformation. The undead saw this as a sign of her readiness.
maedra.wants: Unclear even to her — she has quickened into undeath recently and is still becoming what she will be. Zimru sees her as anchoring 'life beyond death.' She may still carry human attachments.
maedra.does_not_want: To be forgotten by Tagmira. Possibly. She is not yet past caring.
maedra.oddly_also: The undead see her as practically a prophet — her willing transformation has given her unusual status.
maedra.thinks_with: Old knowledge, old grief, old love for the village she came from.
maedra.self_image: In flux. She chose this. She is not sure yet what she chose it for.
maedra.recreation: Unknown. She was last seen weeping at Ildun's grave.
anecdotes: Appeared at Ildun-Ayenar's burial weeping — she knew him better than anyone realized.
Borric of House Caernach saw her on the Bramblewood path at dusk and said she 'looked wrong.'
tragedies: Chose undeath. Whether this is tragedy depends on what she does with it.
maedra.pc_leverage: The party knew her in life. She may be the most human-accessible face of the undead alliance — and also potentially its most dangerous element if her human attachments lead her to act outside Zimru's order.
maedra.dm_notes: Maedra is a slow-burn NPC. She does not need to appear this session necessarily, but her presence should be felt — the undead's deference to her, the way she's changed. She and Zimru are consorts in whatever sense that word applies to the undead.

enni_the_ash_wife.home: Lean-to at the forest's edge, Tagmira
enni_the_ash_wife.appearance: Old, nearly blind, sharp-eyed despite it. Speaks in riddles and feigns more power than she has — though she has some.
enni_the_ash_wife.and_yet: Villagers fear her and also bring her food and trinkets for healing. She accepts both without comment.
enni_the_ash_wife.wants: To pass on her full knowledge before she dies. To name one last heir who will listen.
enni_the_ash_wife.does_not_want: Her people to forget the gods' silence — that forgetting is its own danger.
enni_the_ash_wife.oddly_also: Wants someone to ask her what she actually knows, rather than what she'll say for free.
enni_the_ash_wife.family: A few distant kin and one loyal servant.
enni_the_ash_wife.self_image: The last woman in Tagmira who remembers what it felt like when the gods still spoke.
enni_the_ash_wife.recreation: Tracing stories in the ash of the hearth.
enni_the_ash_wife.dreams: That the hearth-fires will speak again.
anecdotes: Can cast friends and identify — the village knows this and uses her accordingly.
tragedies: Has watched the sacred knowledge of the old ways dwindle to almost nothing in her lifetime.
enni_the_ash_wife.pc_leverage: Respects memory, ritual, and those who listen more than they speak. She almost certainly knows something is wrong with Maedra and the Bramblewood — but will make the party work for it.

tarrek_veyran.appearance: Gruff, broad, soot-stained. Runs the village forge. Fair in his dealings and known for it.
tarrek_veyran.and_yet: Officiates disputes when Hadran is not available — not because he wants to, but because people trust him.
tarrek_veyran.wants: A well-run forge, a village that leaves him alone to work.
tarrek_veyran.does_not_want: The chaos of clan feuds spilling into his hall.
tarrek_veyran.oddly_also: Scoffed at Ildun-Ayenar's funeral that 'even river-priests die thirsty' — he believes in the gods even less than most, which makes him oddly clear-eyed.
tarrek_veyran.family: Elder sister Morla (healer/wise-woman), apprentices Garrin and Felmar.
tarrek_veyran.self_image: A craftsman who keeps the village functional while others argue.
tarrek_veyran.dreams: Nothing as grand as most people's. A good apprentice.
anecdotes: Served as neutral witness at the Trial by Combat between Tenn Darnel and Ulan Caernach.
tarrek_veyran.pc_leverage: Practical help — metalwork, repairs, information about village disputes. Does not deal in rumors but will speak plainly if asked directly.

peema.appearance: Young, quiet, clever. One of the orphan foundlings raised by Brennar Hallan as messengers and servants. Wary of strangers — except Trebastos, with whom she has struck up an unlikely rapport.
peema.and_yet: Carries a small hand mirror that she is never without. She does not explain it.
peema.wants: To understand what the fairy in the mirror is and what it wants from her. She is not frightened of it — she is curious, which may be more dangerous.
peema.does_not_want: Adults taking her mirror. Adults dismissing what she's seen.
peema.oddly_also: Peema says the trees whisper at night. She is not wrong.
peema.thinks_with: Observation and silence. She watches more than she speaks.
peema.family: Orphan — no known family. Raised in House Hallan with Salla and Dun.
peema.self_image: Someone who knows something important that no one will believe.
peema.recreation: Watching. Listening. Long conversations with the mirror when she thinks no one is near.
peema.dreams: Unclear — possibly shaped by whatever the fairy has told her.
anecdotes: Trebastos (Andre's fighter) has struck up a genuine rapport with her — she trusts him in a way she trusts almost no one.
The mirror shows her a fairy — connection to the Gloaming Court and a figure named Aislith, not yet developed.
tragedies: Orphaned under unspecified circumstances. Raised as a servant-ward, not a daughter.
peema.pc_leverage: Trebastos has her trust. She will speak to him about the mirror if he asks directly and privately. She will not speak about it in front of others she doesn't know.
peema.dm_notes: The mirror and the fairy (Gloaming Court / Aislith connection) are an undeveloped thread. Do not force it this session — but Trebastos's relationship with her is a natural way to surface it when ready. The fact that Peema says the trees whisper at night and is correct about it should register to any PC paying attention. The Pact-Render fey and the Gloaming Court may be on opposite sides of the same fey politics — unknown as yet.

overview.what_it_is: A functioning undead temple-complex preserving the rites and hierarchy of the old gods, now led by Zimru, a 300-year-old undead hierophant. Contains the Sphere — a divine communion device now powered by sacrifice.
overview.why_it_exists: Built in the age of the old gods; its undead inhabitants refused to abandon it when the gods went silent. The Bramblewood was grown by the old gods as a living perimeter.
overview.current_state: Functional and alert. The first orc sacrifice was completed; the Sphere is active. Zimru will press the party for more sacrifices. Maedra has newly joined as Zimru's consort and is regarded as a prophet by the undead. The undead know about the Bronze Host threat but cannot project force outside the Bramblewood.
overview.historical_context: The temple served willing sacrifices to Enki, Ninhursag, and the Annunaki pantheon. The undead here are former priests and initiates who chose to remain as custodians. See the Ghoul Theologian's lecture for their theological self-justification.

physical: Hollow Boars patrol near the temple
magical: Black Cantors suppress divine magic in 30' radius
political: The undead recognize the party as allies — but Zimru will judge any breach of the alliance severely
access_challenges.temporal: The undead lose consciousness when straying from the temple unless they pay a high magical cost — they cannot escort the party or intervene outside

environmental_hazards: Torch of the Last Vigil (undead tracking)
unknown_threats: Velric's warning: 'Zimru-Irkala has foreseen it — in the Sphere — YOU ARE IN GREAT DANGER'

treasure: Eight Jars in Necromancy Chamber (including Black Blood/Potion of Healing, Stoneflower Extract/Potion of Undead Control, Distilled Marrow/Potion of Extra-Healing)
knowledge: Sphere operation (Book 5: Who Knees Before the Mantle Gold)
magical_items: Chamber of the Cultivar ritual diagram (Manual of Golems, lignin)
strategic_value: Alliance with Zimru provides intelligence via the Sphere
Undead cannot help against orcs directly but can advise
Maedra may be able to relay information about Tagmira
reputation_gains: Continued good standing with Zimru if sacrifice obligations are met

zimru_and_undead.goals: Keep the Sphere running via sacrifice. Recruit more Tagmirans to undeath. Preserve the temple. Guide what remains of humanity from the Bramblewood.

bronze_host_orcs.goals: Avoid the Bramblewood. The orcs fear it and give it wide berth — one reason they push northwest rather than directly at Tagmira.
bronze_host_orcs.knowledge_level: They know something terrible lives there

common_rumors: The Bramblewood walks at night — shadows that move wrong.
Anyone who enters the wood alone doesn't come back the same.
Old Maedra went in and never came out.

enni_the_ash_wife.cost_to_acquire: Genuine respect and something she values
enni_the_ash_wife.accuracy: High — she has lived near it her whole life
information: The wood was not always like this
Something in there remembers the old gods
Maedra knew more than she ever said
Safest route to the inner sanctum

forbidden_knowledge: The Sphere shows Zimru things — including a great danger coming for the party (Velric's warning). What this danger is has not yet been revealed.

zimru_will_want: A full account of the orc sacrifice — who, how, what rites were observed
Commitment to arranging a second sacrifice. The orcs northwest of Tagmira are the obvious target — this aligns with the party's own interest in stopping the Bronze Host
Information about what the party observed in Caelrun and on the road
current_session_hooks.maedra_factor: The party will encounter her transformed for the first time (or again, if they have already). Her reception of them will be complex — she chose this, but she remembers them.
current_session_hooks.sphere_revelation: Zimru may show the party something in the Sphere — either the Bronze Host's movements, or the vague danger Velric warned about, depending on what serves the story.

bronze_host_territory.name: Bronze Host Orc Territory (Northwest Hunting Grounds)
bronze_host_territory.location: Northwest of Tagmira, beyond the forest hunting grounds

overview.what_it_is: The territory the Bronze Host orcs are seizing from Tagmira's hunters, backed by Pact-Render fey who supply them with bronze weapons and equipment via the Bronzeworking Triangle.
overview.why_it_exists: The Pact-Render has made a bargain with the orc tribe — bronze weapons and equipment in exchange for territorial expansion. Control of the hunting grounds is the first step toward an assault on Tagmira.
overview.current_state: Two months since the party drove back the initial raid. All named orc leaders survived. Equipment significantly upgraded: bronze spears, laminated wooden armor. The Bronzeworking Triangle (Ore Pit, Crucible Glen, Mule Paths) is operational. The undead know but cannot act.
overview.historical_context: These orcs were already territorial but lacked the equipment and coordination for a sustained campaign. The Pact-Render fey changed that equation.

creatures: Orc Warriors of the Bronze Host (HD 1+1, bronze spears, coordinated thrust)
Orc Champions (HD 3, magical fey-carved spears, command voice)
Brat-Kho the Voice That Bends (Script-Stone of Persuasion)
Ulzhar Bone-Tongue (Pendant of the Hollow Tongue)
Wane-Faced overseers at the Ore Pit
Possible Shee emissary on the Mule Paths
traps: Ambush positions in the ravine terrain
Hidden cache sites on the Mule Paths
magical_hazards: Fey bronze weapons ignore cold iron wards
Pact-Render shee have unknown fey powers
unknown_threats: The Shee emissary's capabilities and schedule
Whether the Pact-Render itself can be engaged diplomatically or only opposed

treasure: Relic items carried by the three champions
Possible contents of the Bronzeworking Triangle caches
strategic_value: Destroying or disrupting the Bronzeworking Triangle would collapse the orc military advantage
Eliminating the orc leaders would break the Host's coordination
Capturing the Shee emissary could provide leverage against the Pact-Render
reputation_gains: Tagmira's gratitude and trust if the hunting grounds are secured
Potential second sacrifice for Zimru if orc leaders are taken alive or killed appropriately

tagmira_militia.goals: Recover the northwest hunting grounds before winter. Do not yet grasp the Pact-Render dimension.
tagmira_militia.resources_committed: Hadran can muster 10 militia + 6 veterans if convinced the threat is serious
tagmira_militia.knowledge_level: Knows orcs are better equipped than before. Does not know about the Bronzeworking Triangle or the Shee.

zimru_and_undead.goals: The orcs make excellent sacrifice candidates. Cannot act directly. Will strongly encourage the party to address the threat — it serves both Tagmira and the Sphere.
zimru_and_undead.knowledge_level: High — the Sphere shows them the orc movements

pact_render.goals: Use the orcs as proxies to pressure Tagmira. Keep the Bronzeworking Triangle secret and operational.
pact_render.resources_committed: Shee emissary, bronze supply chain, Wane-Faced overseers
pact_render.knowledge_level: Full awareness of their own operation; does not know about the undead alliance

hex_1_ore_pit.description: Rocky hills with copper and tin extraction. Slave pens cut into stone. Wane-Faced (ogre) overseers and orc enforcers. Minor fey shrine to maintain the Pact.

hex_2_crucible_glen.description: Forested ravine with hidden charcoal kilns and clay forges. Portune-controlled, ritualized. Engraving stations where Pact symbols are inscribed on weapons.

hex_3_mule_paths.description: Foggy pine forest with disguised pack trails and hidden caches of bronze weapons.

captive_witness_incident.summary: The party previously defeated a Bronze Host force and took two orc captives to Tagmira to witness the Trial by Combat at the Circle of Cossus. The intent was intimidation — show the orcs what Tagmira does to its enemies.
captive_witness_incident.possible_backfire: The captives observed Tagmira's current defenses, population, and the fact that the village is relying on outside adventurers. This intelligence, brought back to the Bronze Host, may have encouraged rather than deterred their escalation.
captive_witness_incident.dm_notes: The party is partly responsible for the Bronze Host's current boldness, however inadvertently. Zimru, watching through the Sphere, almost certainly noticed this.

waystone_stop.location: Waystone of Isarna, west of Tagmira (on the road from Caelrun)
waystone_stop.event: Taziri will want to stop for an offering. Bread and silver thread are traditional. Good moment for character roleplay and transition into Tagmira.
waystone_stop.dm_notes: Let this breathe — it signals arrival is near and gives the caravan a moment of ritual normalcy before the complications of Tagmira begin.

flavor_notes: The road north narrows. The Bramblewood is visible as a dark mass on the horizon well before Tagmira itself.
Taziri knows Tagmira well — she can brief the party on surface village politics if asked.
The militia members who know the party might be met on the road or at the gate.
Nothing in the Bramblewood moves by day — but the sense of being watched from the tree line is hard to shake.

possible_road_encounter.name: Returning Patrol
possible_road_encounter.description: A Tagmira patrol of 3 militia heading back from the northwest. They are not fleeing — they held the line but were outmatched. One is bandaged. They know the party and will report directly: the orcs have bronze weapons, they move in coordinated groups, and there are more of them than last spring. They want to know if the party is coming to help.
possible_road_encounter.dm_notes: Sets the tone correctly — these are not frightened villagers, they are soldiers who fought and are thinking about how to fight better. The party's arrival matters to them.

immediate: Zimru wants an accounting of the sacrifice and a commitment to the next one — the orcs northwest are the obvious candidates
Tagmira's hunters are being pushed off the northwest grounds — Hadran needs help or intelligence
Maedra's transformation — the party will face her changed, and the village still doesn't know she's gone
slow_burn: The undead want Tagmiran recruits — someone in the village may already be in contact with them
The dryad in the Tree of Mist-Song — bitterly lonely, has knowledge of the Bramblewood, waiting
Velric's warning from the Sphere — a great danger foreseen, not yet revealed
Tenn Darnel lost the duel and has not forgiven it — a grudge waiting for a trigger
session_pressure_points.convergence: The orc threat and the sacrifice demand point in the same direction — the party can satisfy Zimru and defend Tagmira with the same action, if they can coordinate militia, manage the undead alliance's secrecy, and get through the Bronze Host's now-upgraded defenses.
